item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Those mirror tests are unnecessary but man, that music is beautiful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know it's a bit necro but…
I like this but, as others have mentioned, I don't see the point of repeating the mirror tests. Just seems pointlessly cruel. Would be better if some of those tests were replaced with tests on exposure to religious items or something.

+1 tho

Edit: oh and I feel dumb for not getting the angel thing. I actually started thinking "living guitar".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No cost too great. No mind to think. No will to break. No voice to cry suffering. You will seal the light that plagues their dreams. You are the vessel. You are the Hollow Knight.
This reminds me of Hollow Knight, with both the name of the SCP and the screaming. Neat, but I can only imagine it as the Hollow Knight.

item.title: Re: Regarding the recent page revision
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the heads up. I obviously haven't maintained this page in years so it's good to see someone still had it in mind to take care of it :)

item.title: 5 years later...
item.content:encoded:
I randomly decided to check this website today. I think conceptually I still like my idea, but it's definitely gonna need some work in a lot of areas. I can tell this is dated and my high-school writing skills weren't as great as they could be.

Just for funzies I'll give it some work.

Glad to see people still even see this in 2018! Hope it ends up being something more people like once I have time to touch it up.

item.title: Regarding the recent page revision
item.content:encoded: Just an FYI, I went ahead and changed the audio player from Flash to HTML5, as Flash is rapidly losing support and already can't be viewed by many people, including Firefox and Mobile users. No content changes were made, and the article is otherwise unchanged.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Exactly my point as well. There seems to be no reason whatsoever to specifically test this sorry creature with mirror cell confinement for an increasing amount of time. The whole test log reeks of uncreative stench.

Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't get it until I read the comments, that's bad. I read it knowing this and I think I still wouldn't get in any way unless I read the comments, that's extra bad. The idea is cool, but the execution leaves much to be desired. The "test" (if you can call that thing a test) seemed cruel and pointless. Kinda like "Hey, this thing don't like when we do this stuff… what if we do this stuff for a long time?! Huh!? Me smarts! Me most smarts than all!". Downvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is perfection. The appearance for once isn't based on other art, but the account of Jacob when he wrestled the angel.

item.title: Upvoted, but...
item.content:encoded:
It doesn't try to communicate, but utilizes those specific appendages for communicating? I could be missing something here. Yerp, I'm a bit of a fool, it CAN use them to, but doesn't. Eck.

I love how simple this is, leaves some interpretation to be had and just gives a nice "poor creature" vibe.
May want some more diverse tests though, traumatizing the thing via presenting it's altered existence five different times? Interesting how it actively destroys containment without harming itself in the process, but yeah, just cruel as everyone else is saying.

Maybe expose it to new music, or document reactions to different religious bodies/representations.

(Mild spit balls to be taken with a grain of salt. Bottom line: love it, +1)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding this. This article must have changed a good bit since I first saw it, and now it seems like we're just torturing this thing for no reason. I mean, all that comes out of these test are broken testing chambers and an unconscious skip. What's the point?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't really see why the foundation would subject the creature to distress when it can clearly see it's distress response decibel level increases linearly with the exposure time, especially seeing how decibels are an exponential unit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for the creepy sad cherubim. reminds me of a wind in the door.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
any weaponry off-site at all times in order

Sharp objects are to be contained in locked storage.

That all seems like a bit much if this thing is just to be contained in a cell most of the time. No weapons on site? Seems like it'd just be easier to keep them out of reach/sight whenever the being is moved/interacted with.

Also, the test logs seem a bit repetitive to me. 5 minutes to 15 minutes don't seem to add much beside slow down the reader, imho.

Edit: Music is a nice touch btw.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very nice. You've done a good job of giving the clues needed, but keeping it subtle. The fact that nobody in the comments has followed the SCP's narrative past "angel" bodes well for the fridge logic value.
I am assuming that

+ [Spoilers]
- Close

[if it fell from the sky with wings burned off, reacts negatively to religious items, hates to look at itself, and is suicidal, it may no longer be an "angel" per se…]

Of course, I might be wrong, and I'm sure I'm not the first to notice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awesome :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually created that piece using a basic synth keyboard and editing it in audacity.

I call it "As She Sleeps," in reference to my ex-girlfriend who has a thing for lucid dreaming. It was intended to be dreamlike, hence the name.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, I meant where did you get it from? And other than as a reference to this article, does it have a name?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's the "musical number" that 1708 plays to itself in containment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just curious, but what is that audio file at the end?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed the imperial-metric measurements.

Welllp, can't please everyone I s'pose

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad you liked it!

Yes, I created that track :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
[…] located ████ feet […]

I didn't find this interesting conceptually. There's nothing here beyond a weird-looking creature. If it's an angel, the implications of that could have been explored more. The whole thing with HI doesn't really add much. Emotionally torturing the thing to see how loud it can scream seems cruel and pointless; I wouldn't mind the cruelty as much if it weren't so pointless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is just weird and creepy: exactly what an SCP should be. Also, I can't believe I didn't realize it was supposed to be an angel until I checked the comments.

The sound file was a nice touch. Did you make it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why would they even try to use sedatives on an organism made entirely out of metals?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a lot of awkwardly worded parts, and I found the containment procedures to be too short. Also, the tests just seem unnecessarily cruel. No vote, but leaning towards downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll find some way to elaborate on the reflection bit without revealing too much information, thanks for the feedback

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's an interesting looking organism, but overall not that interesting. The part that intrigued me was the bit about the mirror, but it doesn't lead to anything. Also, the audio is very obviously synthesized, it doesn't sound like anything that could come from a living being. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm excited to have my first positive entry, haha

Thank you! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And we're back! In the positives, this time, too. You really did a great job turning this thing around.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you very much!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great improvement over your last try. You nailed the sympathy angle without clubbing the reader over the head with it. I'm impressed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As far as the Foundation is aware, SCP-1708 does not respond to human sedatives in any way.

Very glad you liked it :) I'm actually in the process of writing a tale regarding this SCP, and I'll let you know when I finish it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
resulting in armed combat lasting approximately ███ hours.

Damn, a three figures number of hours?

Not a bad "damn", you know. Just "damn."

Sedatives do not have an effect on this behavior

Did they have any effect on any other behavior? Not trying to be picky here, it's not a critic, just curious if its physiology is compatible with our sedatives. Or did they use any other kind of molecule that happens to act as a sedative for 1708?

All in all, like the cool concept. +1 and wishing to see more about the Cithara advenus adflictus.

Edit: Also, the Test Log 1708-C would be probably a good addition! : D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm very glad you like it, thank you :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Solid idea, and the music is a very nice touch. I'd say that's worth an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I will definitely see what I can do to organize it more, thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same feeling, except I'll upvote it for potential.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like what you have going on here conceptually and narratively, but the writing is rather disorganized — it took me about two tries to get through any given passage. No vote for now, because I have confidence that this could be pretty good with some more work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(Spoiler alert) Good call brother, that was the intention completely! Harp-noises, wings, shininess, the works…

I honestly feel like it's missing something too, but if I think of something I'll be sure to throw it in :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So… it's some sort of wounded angel?

It feels like it's missing something, but I find myself intrigued nonetheless - there are a lot of unanswered questions, and in a good kind of way.
